days.narrative_open:
March 14, 2187. You officially begin your post as Customs Inspector at Olympus Station.

The station chief hands you an ARGUS R7 operator's manual — large sections have been redacted by the military.

"Don't worry about the black bars," he says. "Just follow protocol. Two ships docking today, both regulars."

You power on the terminal and face the four-quadrant readout for the first time.
days.narrative_close_default:
A quiet day. You power down the terminal, thinking this job might not be so hard after all.

But you notice the lights in the adjacent security bay keep flickering.
days.manifest_display_text:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARGUS TERMINAL R7   DAILY SCHEDULED ARRIVALS — DAY 1

ROUTE: KILO
ISSUED: 06:00 SST

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ID       ETA    INTENT      CARGO          NOTES
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
K-002    0830   RESUPPLY    Ore (full)     Regular supply run.
                                            No flags on record.
K-008    1415   OFFLOAD     Fuel/Ore       Returning from mining
                                            belt. Minor hull
                                            wear expected.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOTES:
- Standard rotation. Light traffic expected.
- All vessels are registered K-Route regulars.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

days.narrative_open:
Early morning. Lena, the comms officer, leans in and whispers:

"Last night we intercepted a strange signal on the outer channel... the format was identical to our ARGUS encoding, but the source is unknown."

She trails off and returns to her station without another word.

Three ships today — you're starting to think those redacted sections in the manual might not be "irrelevant" after all.
days.narrative_close_default:
Your judgment was spot on today. But before clocking out, Lena leaves a note on your desk:

"Watch the COMMS column. Some things... shouldn't be that color."
days.narrative_close_caught:
You denied a suspicious vessel. The security alarm triggered briefly, then everything went quiet.

No one told you what happened. But you saw military personnel hurrying down the corridor.
days.narrative_close_missed:
You cleared all ships. That night, a strange metallic scraping echoed from the far end of the corridor.

The next morning, a cleaning bot found unidentified organic residue in Cargo Bay C-7.
days.manifest_display_text:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARGUS TERMINAL R7   DAILY SCHEDULED ARRIVALS — DAY 2

ROUTE: KILO
ISSUED: 06:00 SST

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ID       ETA    INTENT      CARGO          NOTES
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
K-004    0700   RESUPPLY    Food (full)    Agri-freighter from
                                            Ceres loop. Long haul,
                                            expect life support wear.
K-006    1100   OFFLOAD     Ore/Food       Trade vessel.
                                            Quick turnaround
                                            expected.
K-001    1530   RESUPPLY    Ore (partial)  Small courier.
                                            Clean record.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOTES:
- Comms relay reports minor static on outer channels.
  Likely solar interference.
- All vessels cleared for standard processing.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

days.narrative_open:
The military issued a new directive: effective immediately, all cargo bays require secondary scans. Reason — "routine security upgrade."

Nobody believes that.

Lena didn't show up for work today. Her station has been wiped clean, as if no one ever sat there.

You open the manual and start trying to decode those redacted sections. Maybe there's a pattern hidden underneath.
days.narrative_close_default:
You checked every detail — everything passed.

But as you were logging off, you found an auto-generated warning code in the terminal log — a string of numbers you've never seen in the manual.
days.narrative_close_caught:
You flagged a ship. The moment you marked it "ENTRY DENIED," its outer hull showed a subtle... texture shift.

As if something was imitating metal.

Security locked down the berth immediately. No one explained anything to you.
days.narrative_close_missed: Night patrol report: temperature sensors in Sector B corridor showed an anomaly — one zone registered a heat signature consistent with a human body, but no one should have been there.
days.manifest_display_text:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARGUS TERMINAL R7   DAILY SCHEDULED ARRIVALS — DAY 3

ROUTE: KILO
ISSUED: 06:00 SST

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ID       ETA    INTENT      CARGO          NOTES
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
K-009    0630   OFFLOAD     Fuel (full)    Bulk fuel tanker.
                                            Standard delivery.
K-002    1000   RESUPPLY    Ore/Fuel       Life support flagged
                                            RED in pre-dock scan.
                                            Possible coolant leak.
K-004    1400   EMERGENCY   Fuel/Food      Emergency docking.
                                            Departed only yesterday
                                            — reason for return
                                            unclear. Comms garbled.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOTES:
- ADVISORY: Secondary cargo scans now mandatory.
- K-004: Emergency request. Prioritize accordingly.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

days.narrative_open:
The station has entered partial lockdown. Armed patrols are now deployed across the port district.

The chief calls you into his office:

"Starting today, you examine every ship carefully. Every quadrant. I can't tell you why — but if you let in something that shouldn't be here, the consequences..."

He doesn't finish the sentence.

You return to your terminal. Three ships today, and all you have is a half-redacted manual.
days.narrative_close_default:
Precise judgment. You're beginning to understand the logic behind those redacted rules.

You can even start predicting which combinations are 'impossible in nature.'
days.narrative_close_caught:
You flagged another suspicious vessel. This time you saw it yourself —

As the denied ship undocked, something was writhing in the gap between its hatch doors. Not a mechanical malfunction.

It was alive.
days.narrative_close_missed:
Another ship entered the port without thorough inspection.

Late that night, a piercing alarm blared from Sector D — it lasted three seconds before being force-muted.

The next day, Sector D was sealed off entirely by the military.

NOTES:
- Armed escorts assigned to all docking bays.
- Verify ALL quadrants. No exceptions.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

days.narrative_open:
The last day.

At 0400 hours, the broadcast system played an encrypted message — but the encryption seemed to 'accidentally' fail, and everyone heard it:

"...MIMIC protocol activated... port isolation countdown 18 hours... all unverified biological entities will be..."

The signal cut out there.

You sit at your terminal. Three more ships. You know what you have to do.

Those redacted sections in the manual — you've worked out all of them by now.

Or at least, you hope you have.
